metadata.description: A test home page for 78th Street Studios built from existing public site copy and structure.

components.headline: Northeast Ohio's eclectic arts maze
components.subheadline: A test remake of the current 78th Street Studios site, centered on the Cleveland arts complex, Third Fridays, and event rentals.

components.title: What the current site says matters most

items.title: Largest fine arts complex
items.body: The home page describes 78th Street Studios as the largest fine arts complex in Northeast Ohio, with more than 60 galleries, studios, and creative businesses.

items.title: Third Fridays
items.body: The current site promotes a monthly Third Fridays event and, as of March 28, 2026, lists the next date as April 17, 2026.

items.title: Venue and rental use
items.body: The public messaging leans hard on private events and weddings, not just artist studios, so the test page keeps that dual use visible.

components.headline: Visit, explore, or book the building
components.body: This sample keeps the strongest actions from the live site: learn the backstory, view the building, or follow the event and venue paths.

metadata.description: A test about page for 78th Street Studios based on the live site's history, event, and building summary.

components.headline: A 170,000-square-foot arts and design complex in Cleveland
components.subheadline: The live About page mixes building history, artist access, monthly public programming, and venue rentals. This sample condenses that into a cleaner two-page structure.

components.title: How the existing About page describes the place

items.title: What it is
items.body: 78th Street Studios presents itself as a 170,000-square-foot complex of galleries, artist studios, performance spaces, and music recording studios.

items.title: Public access
items.body: Viewing hours are framed as free and open to the public, with the strongest recurring invitation centered on the monthly Third Fridays event.

items.title: Third Fridays
items.body: The live copy describes Third Fridays as a multisensory event with visual art, ambient music, food, and pop-up vendors across four floors.

items.title: 1905 origins
items.body: The building dates to 1905 and originally housed the Baker Electric Motor Vehicle Company, which explains the industrial shell that still shapes the experience.

items.title: Creative reuse
items.body: The site highlights later use by American Greetings' creative studios, reinforcing the building's shift from manufacturing to long-term creative production.

items.title: Regional draw
items.body: Much of the About page is social proof: awards, notable tenants, music recorded onsite, and the broader Gordon Square connection.

components.headline: The current content is strong, but the structure is carrying too much
components.body: This test page shows the content can be reorganized into a simpler home-plus-about flow, though the framework still needs richer content blocks for long-form storytelling.
